LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YIFENG GAO'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO ORANGE COUNTY AND PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO REMIT ALL REQUIRED TRANSFER COSTS. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. (CODE CIV. PROC. 396B.) PLAINTIFF HAD A GOOD FAITH BASIS FOR FILING THIS ACTION IN SAN FRANCISCO BECAUSE THAT WAS DEFENDANT'S LAST KNOWN ADDRESS. (FINWALL DECL., 3-4; LAM DECL., 10, 13.) IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF WAS WILLING TO STIPULATE TO THE TRANSFER BUT DEFENDANT IGNORED PLAINTIFF'S COMMUNICATIONS. (FINWALL DECL., 8-10, 13-14, 16-18.) THE COURT INSTRUCTS DEFENSE COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WHICH REPEATS THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO, CSR#8600, E-MAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-08-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defts To Transfer Venue; Memo Of P&A; Supporting Declarations
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT M. GRANDON, ESQ. of Demidchik Law Firm (Tel: 415-960-3333) appearing for defendant Yifeng Gao;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT YIFENG GAO's Motion By Defendant To Transfer Venue.

Argument presented as to attorney's fees. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Yifeng Gao's motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to Orange County and plaintiff is required to remit all required transfer costs. The court denies defendant's request for attorney's fees. (Code Civ. Proc. 396b.) Plaintiff had a good faith basis for filing this action in San Francisco because that was defendant's last known address. (Finwall Decl., 3-4; Lam Decl., 10, 13.) In addition, plaintiff was willing to stipulate to the transfer but defendant ignored plaintiff's communications. (Finwall Decl., 8-10, 13-14, 16-18.)

The Court instructs defense counsel to submit a proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org which repeats the court's ruling verbatim.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
